svg.text: exact landing on N — game over
Builder — board setup · Strategy — dice (prod vs deterministic) · Observer — game events
jumps stored as HashMap<cell, target> — no scan, and the chain re-check reuses the same lookup

g.text: EXACT LANDING TO WIN — the classic edge case:
if pos + roll > N the player overshoots and STAYS PUT — turn just ends.
you can only win by landing EXACTLY on the last cell.
interviewers love the off-by-one here — state it before they ask.
a ladder top may itself be a snake head (or vice-versa) → re-check after each jump.
loop back to the snake/ladder test until the cell is stable.
cap at 20 hops — a mis-configured board could otherwise loop forever.
duplicate snake heads / out-of-bounds cells fail at build(), not mid-game.
playTurn() is synchronized — safe if turns arrive from web-socket threads.
FixedDiceStrategy makes the whole loop deterministic for tests.

svg.text: ① current player rolls → ② overshoot? stay put : move → ③ resolve snake / ladder, re-check the chain → ④ landed on N? WINS : next player ↻

g.text: Snake & Ladder — LLD (turn loop + chain resolution)
